63be318bb3c05525875977d2.leadin: This work’s main character enters the title location after his village is attacked by slavers and he is separated from his brother, who rescues him at this book’s end. For 10 points each:
63be318bb3c05525875977d2.leadin_sanitized: This work's main character enters the title location after his village is attacked by slavers and he is separated from his brother, who rescues him at this book's end. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this work whose narrator encounters such characters as the Flash-eyed Mother in the thirteenth town and a creature that demands the narrator lick her sores for ten years to be allowed to go home.
My Life in the Bush of Ghosts is a novel by this Nigerian author, who wrote of a man who goes on a quest to find his tapster in The Palm-Wine Drinkard.

In The Palm-Wine Drinkard, the tapster is eventually located in this a place know as this entity's “town.” This four-letter term describes the condition of Mr. Kurtz as relayed by a servant to Marlow in Heart of Darkness.
